title: Scientists Measure Powerful Jets from Black Holes
summary: Scientists have measured the power of jets coming from a black hole for the first time. They used a network of radio telescopes around the world to study a black hole called Cygnus X-1. This black hole is in a system with a big star. The jets are streams of energy that shoot out from the black hole. The scientists found that these jets are very powerful, with energy equal to 10,000 Suns. They also saw that the jets move at half the speed of light. The star's strong winds push the jets, making them dance around. This helps scientists understand how black holes affect space and galaxies. The research was done by a team from Curtin University and other places.

background_read: Black holes are mysterious objects in space with strong gravity that can pull in everything, even light. They are often found in galaxies and can have jets of energy shooting out from them. Scientists study black holes to learn how they shape the universe. Telescopes help them see these faraway objects. This article talks about a new discovery where scientists measured how powerful these jets are for the first time, using a special setup of telescopes.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from Curtin University and other places
WHAT: Measured the power of jets from a black hole for the first time
WHERE: In space, focusing on the Cygnus X-1 system
WHY: To understand how black holes influence galaxies and space

perspectives.description: This shows how new tools help scientists learn more about space and black holes.
